_readme: Central MCP server catalog — COLD STORAGE ONLY. Nothing here is executed directly; entries are templates copied into a project's .mcp.json by the /add-mcp skill (or by hand). Audit scripts that look for active/dead MCPs should ignore this file. Entries with {{PLACEHOLDERS}} require substitution at inject time.
_policy: Do not delete entries even if globally inactive — they exist for re-injection on demand. Do not auto-prune. Top-level keys: _readme, _policy, catalog (the actual entry map keyed by short name).

github.description: GitHub API — read/write repos, issues, PRs, branches, files
github.when: Any project that interacts with GitHub repos, issues, or PRs

vercel.description: Vercel platform — list/inspect deployments, logs, env vars, domains, projects

mongodb.description: MongoDB — query, aggregate, inspect schemas and indexes (custom connection string)
mongodb.scopeNote: Project-local .mcp.json or the mongosh CLI only — never user/global scope. A global UNPINNED entry caused recurring 'Invalid Version' MCP-failed-to-connect errors (removed 2026-06-09). Keep mongodb per-project and pinned.
mongodb.when: Projects using MongoDB — substitute MONGO_URI with the actual connection string

mongodb-local.description: MongoDB on localhost:27017 — preset, no substitution needed
mongodb-local.scopeNote: Project-local .mcp.json or the mongosh CLI only — never user/global scope (see 'mongodb' entry; global unpinned mongodb caused recurring MCP failures, removed 2026-06-09).
mongodb-local.when: Local development with a MongoDB instance running on the default port

redis.description: Redis — get/set keys, scan, sorted sets, hashes (custom connection string)
redis.when: Projects using Redis — substitute REDIS_URL with the actual connection string

redis-local.description: Redis on localhost:6379 — preset, no substitution needed
redis-local.when: Local development with a Redis instance running on the default port

postgres.description: PostgreSQL — query tables, inspect schema (custom connection string)
postgres.when: Projects using PostgreSQL — substitute POSTGRES_URL with the actual connection string

postgres-local.description: PostgreSQL on localhost:5432/postgres — preset, no substitution needed
postgres-local.when: Local development with a Postgres instance running on the default port

aws.description: AWS core MCP — interact with AWS services via the default profile
aws.when: Projects that deploy to or interact with AWS infrastructure

render.description: Render.com — list/manage services, deploys, databases, env vars

svelte.description: Svelte docs MCP — official Svelte/SvelteKit documentation, playground links, autofixer
svelte.scopeNote: Add to .mcp.json only — never user-scope (injects mandatory context into every session)

vscode.description: VS Code state MCP — access open files, workspace state from VS Code extension
vscode.when: When running Claude from within VS Code and needing to read editor state

scratchpad.description: Scratchpad MCP — read/write/query/summarize local and global scratchpad entries for plans, learnings, and cross-project references
scratchpad.when: All projects — provides structured working memory for agents

shell-mem.description: diy-claude-mem shell tracking MCP server — tail, search, mark-done, cleanup, append
shell-mem.when: All projects — tracks shell command history and background processes

file-tools.description: File tools MCP — read/write/convert CSV, Excel, JSON, YAML, TOML, XML with output shaping and HTTP client
file-tools.when: Projects that need to read, write, transform, or convert data files (CSV, Excel, JSON, YAML, TOML, XML) or make HTTP requests

crontab.description: System crontab MCP — list, add, remove, and validate cron entries with human-readable schedule parsing
crontab.when: When you need structured crontab management instead of raw shell commands. Build signal: repeated crontab operations in a session.
crontab.notes: Currently unnecessary — crontab -l / echo | crontab - via Bash covers all cases. Build when the shell approach becomes friction.

processes.description: System process MCP — list, inspect, kill processes and check port occupancy with structured output
processes.when: When you need structured process management instead of ps/kill/lsof shell commands. Build signal: repeated process debugging in a session.
processes.notes: Currently unnecessary — ps, kill, lsof -i :PORT via Bash covers all cases. pm2 handles named services. Build when shell parsing becomes friction.

interactive-inputs.description: Interactive user input tools — confirm, pick, forms, wizards via MCP elicitation
interactive-inputs.when: Always available — provides rich user input beyond AskUserQuestion (dropdowns, checkboxes, number fields, multi-step wizards)

slack.description: Slack MCP — users / channels / DMs lookup, smart history, search (korotovsky/slack-mcp-server)
slack.scopeNote: Add per-project only — team-scoped tokens shouldn't live at user or global level
slack.when: Projects that need Slack user-ID lookup (e.g., deep-links in docs), channel history, or message search
slack.notes: Bot token (xoxb-) is enough for user + channel lookup. User token (xoxp-) is needed for search + DM read. See https://github.com/korotovsky/slack-mcp-server/blob/master/docs/01-authentication-setup.md
